retry_reason: Put operations release a reference; if the refcount were already zero, calling again would be a use-after-free. Retry is not meaningful.
contract_summary: This BPF kfunc releases a reference to an xfrm_state object by decrementing its refcount. It is a pure put wrapper. After calling this function, the caller must not use the passed x pointer any further, as the object may be freed when the refcount reaches zero. It is intended to be paired with a corresponding get-operation (e.g., bpf_xdp_xfrm_state_acquire) for reference-count management in BPF programs. There is no return value and the operation is unconditional.

judge.reason: The function solely calls xfrm_state_put() to decrement the refcount of the given xfrm_state. No other logic exists.

check.reason: xfrm_state_put(x) decrements the refcount at x->refcnt.refs. The access path x.refcnt.refs and type chain xfrm_state--refcount_t--atomic_t are consistent with the callee info and the code. The critical variable is parameter 1 (x), and the operation is a put on that object.
